title: The Artist of Naboo
content:

|pages=12<ref name="The Artist of Naboo" />
|timeline=Between [[22 BBY/Legends|22]] and [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]]<ref name="Date">"[[The Artist of Naboo]]" was published in ''[[Star Wars Legends Epic Collection: The Clone Wars Vol. 1]]'', which states that the stories within it take place between {{Film|II}} and {{Film|III}}. ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' places the events of these films in [[22 BBY/Legends|22]] and [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]], therefore meaning "The Artist of Naboo" must take place between those [[Standard year/Legends|years]].</ref>
|series=
|preceded by=*"[[Old Wounds]]" {{C|''Star Wars: Visionaries''}}<ref name="Star Wars: Visionaries" />

}}
{{Quote|Just north of Theed, an artist lived and worked in his studio at the base of an abandoned castle.|The opening line of the story|<ref name="The Artist of Naboo" />}}
"'''The Artist of Naboo'''" is a twelve-page [[Star Wars Legends|Legends]] [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] written and illustrated by [[Erik Tiemens]] and published by [[Dark Horse Comics]] in the anthology ''[[Star Wars: Visionaries]]''. Presented as a series of [[Painting/Legends|paintings]], the comic follows an [[Unidentified Naboo artist|artist]] from the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]] who has a series of [[Force vision/Legends|premonitions]] relating to [[Senator/Legends|Senator]] [[Padmé Amidala/Legends|Padmé Amidala]] after encountering her. The artist obsessively paints Amidala for [[Standard week/Legends|weeks]] and ultimately saves her from a [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Separatist]] [[Attack on Theed|attack]]. The comic was created following [[Lucasfilm]] senior editor [[Jonathan W. Rinzler]]'s idea to highlight the works of concept artists who worked on the [[prequel trilogy]] film [[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith|''Star Wars'': Episode III ''Revenge of the Sith'']].

==Publisher's summary==
''"''Evocative paintings capture the tale of an unnamed artist from Naboo, who is captivated by Padmé Amidala after a chance encounter. Her beautiful features graces ''[''sic'']'' his artwork, leading to disturbing premonitions about her future.''"<ref name="Inside Star Wars: Visionaries" />''

==Plot summary==
{{Quote|A chance encounter, and Padmé's gaze transfixed the artist. Unexpected visions of Padmé and her twins replaced his suns & moons.|Narration|<ref name="The Artist of Naboo" />}}
[[File:ArtistPainting.png|thumb|right|200px|The artist becomes consumed with the urge to paint Padmé Amidala.]]
During the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]],<ref name="Legends" /> a reclusive [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Artist/Legends|artist]] works in an [[Abandoned Naboo castle|abandoned castle]] north of [[Theed/Legends|Theed]], the [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[City/Legends|city]] of<ref name="The Artist of Naboo" /> the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]]. He is consumed with [[Painting/Legends|painting]] [[Star/Legends|suns]] and [[Moon/Legends|moons]]. Concurrently, [[Senator/Legends|Senator]] [[Padmé Amidala/Legends|Padmé Amidala]] enjoys the beauty of her [[Garden/Legends|gardens]] on peaceful [[Standard day/Legends|days]]. Both individuals have a chance encounter at the outskirts of Theed, where Amidala's gaze transfixes the artist. Immediately afterward, the artist begins to have visions of Amidala and her<ref name="The Artist of Naboo" /> unborn<ref name="ROTS">{{Film|III}}</ref> [[Twin/Legends|twins]],<ref name="The Artist of Naboo" /> [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] and [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia]],<ref name="ROTS" /> instead of the suns and moons.<ref name="The Artist of Naboo" />

After making his way to the [[Castle/Legends|castle]], the artist obsessively paints Amidala for twenty-four [[Standard week/Legends|weeks]] until he has an epiphany. The artist enters Theed just as it suffers an [[Attack on Theed|attack]] by the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]]. He knows that he needs to find and rescue Amidala. Amidala tries to escape as the artist searches the city streets; he ultimately saves her life by taking [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] fire from [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droids]] meant for her. The artist recovers from his wounds and continues painting based on intuitive visions, with his next focus being<ref name="The Artist of Naboo" /> the planet<ref name="TEA" /> [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]].<ref name="The Artist of Naboo" />

==Development==
{{Quote|"The Artist of Naboo" by Erik Tiemens: An artisan living in the Naboo city of Theed becomes entranced by images of Padmé, leading to disturbing visions of her future.|Description of the story from ''Star Wars Insider''|<ref name="Insider 82">{{InsiderCite|82|Drawn by the Force}}</ref>}}
The twelve-page [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] story "The Artist of Naboo" was written,<ref name="The Artist of Naboo" /> pencilled, inked, lettered, and colored<ref name="Legends" /> by concept design supervisor [[Erik Tiemens]] for the graphic novel anthology ''[[Star Wars: Visionaries]]''. The concept for the graphic novel was pitched by [[Lucasfilm]] senior editor [[Jonathan W. Rinzler]],<ref name="Insider 82" /> who wanted a way to honor the unseen works from artists that had worked on the [[prequel trilogy]] film [[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith|''Star Wars'': Episode III ''Revenge of the Sith'']].<ref name="Star Wars: Visionaries" /> Each artist had free reign to explore any aspect of the ''[[Star Wars]]'' universe of their choosing.<ref name="Comics Companion">''[[Star Wars: The Comics Companion]]''</ref> ''Visionaries'' was announced on [[StarWars.com]] on [[August 3]], [[2004]], in a post which also revealed Tiemens' involvement.<ref name="Visionaries announcement">{{SWArchive|url=eu/lit/comics/news20040803.html|text=Coming Soon: ''Star Wars: Visionaries''}}</ref> A later post on [[March 15]], [[2005]] revealed the titles and summaries of each of the eleven stories in ''Visionaries''.<ref name="Inside Star Wars: Visionaries" /> "The Artist of Naboo" is illustrated as a series of paintings and features only third-person narration instead of dialogue.<ref name="The Artist of Naboo" />

==Release and reception==
[[File:Erik-Tiemens.jpg|thumb|left|Erik Tiemens wrote and painted "The Artist of Naboo."]]
''Visionaries'' was edited by [[Jeremy Barlow]] and published as a trade paperback by [[Dark Horse Comics]] on [[April 2]], 2005,<ref name="Inside Star Wars: Visionaries" /> prior to the premiere of ''Revenge of the Sith'' on [[May 19]] that year.<ref name="Year by Year">''[[Star Wars Year By Year: A Visual History, New Edition]]''</ref> In the anthology, "The Artist of Naboo" is placed second, following "[[Old Wounds]]" and preceeding "[[Wat Tambor and the Quest for the Sacred Eye of the Albino Cyclops]]."<ref name="Star Wars: Visionaries" /> Barlow praised the work of the artists who made ''Visionaries'', stating that they had justified why they were working for<ref name="Insider 82" /> ''Star Wars'' creator<ref name="Year by Year" /> [[George Lucas]].<ref name="Insider 82" /> ''Visionaries'' was later republished by Dark Horse in ''[[Star Wars Omnibus: Wild Space Volume 2]]''<ref name="Omnibus" /> on [[October 9]], [[2013]].<ref name="Dark Horse">{{DarkHorse|url=Books/18-450/Star-Wars-Omnibus-Wild-Space-Volume-2-TPB|text=Star Wars Omnibus: Wild Space Volume 2 TPB}}</ref> "The Artist of Naboo" was later published in ''[[Star Wars Legends Epic Collection: The Clone Wars Vol. 1]]'' by [[Marvel Comics]]<ref name="Legends" /> on [[December 28]], [[2016]].<ref name="Marvel">{{Marvel|url=comics/collection/59777/star_wars_legends_epic_collection_the_clone_wars_vol_1_trade_paperback|text=Star Wars Legends Epic Collection: The Clone Wars Vol. 1 (Trade Paperback)}}</ref> The story occupies the last place in the collection, following the 2004 one-shot comic ''[[Jedi: Yoda]]''.<ref name="Legends" />

==Continuity==
{{Quote|Later… he recovered from laser wounds to continue on, painting intuitive visions… Alderaan concerned him next…|The final line of the story|<ref name="The Artist of Naboo" />}}
[[File:PadmeBazaar-AoN.png|thumb|right|Padmé Amidala, as portrayed in the comic]]
"The Artist of Naboo" focuses on the titular character's fixation on Padmé Amidala,<ref name="The Artist of Naboo" /> a major character in the prequel trilogy films.<ref name="Year by Year" /> The comic includes the titular character's premonition of Luke Skywalker and Leia Organa,<ref name="The Artist of Naboo" /> tying into the plot of ''Revenge of the Sith'', in which Amidala [[Death/Legends|dies]] while [[Reproduction/Legends|giving birth]] to them.<ref name="ROTS" /> The comic also portrays an attack by [[B1-series battle droid/Legends|B1-series battle droids]] in Theed, similar to the [[Capture of Theed/Legends|Capture of Theed]] as portrayed in the [[1999]] prequel [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'']].<ref name="TPM">{{Film|I}}</ref> A Separatist [[Plot to assassinate Senator Amidala/Legends|plot to assassinate Amidala]] was also a a major plotline in the [[2002]] film [[Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones|''Star Wars'': Episode II ''Attack of the Clones'']].<ref name="AOTC">{{Film|II}}</ref> Although the artist is not described as [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]], the concept of visions of the future<ref name="The Artist of Naboo" /> through [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] has been previously portrayed in ''Star Wars'' media, originating with the [[1980]] film [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''Star Wars'': Episode V ''The Empire Strikes Back'']].<ref name="ESB">{{Film|V}}</ref>

The comic ends with the artist's visions turning to Alderaan,<ref name="The Artist of Naboo" /> which [[in-universe]] would later be [[Destruction of Alderaan/Legends|destroyed]] by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire's]] [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]] [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapon]] in [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]]<ref name="TEA" /> as portrayed in the [[1977]] [[original trilogy]] film [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']].<ref name="ANH">{{Film|IV}}</ref> A summary of the stories in ''Visionaries'' published in the [[Drawn by the Force]] department in the [[Star Wars Insider 82|eighty-second issue]] of the ''[[Star Wars Insider]]'' magazine,<ref name="Insider 82" /> which shipped to subscribers on [[May 17]], 2005,<ref name="Insider 82 date">{{SWArchive|url=community/fanclub/mag/news20050506.html|text=The Dark Side Shines in ''Star Wars Insider'' #82}}</ref> incorrectly stated that the artist in the story worked within Theed,<ref name="Insider 82" /> rather than north of it as described in the comic.<ref name="The Artist of Naboo" /> "The Artist of Naboo" is part of ''Star Wars'' Expanded Universe media, which was categorized as [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] in [[2014]].<ref name="Legends announcement">{{SW|url=news/the-legendary-star-wars-expanded-universe-turns-a-new-page|text=The Legendary Star Wars Expanded Universe Turns a New Page}}</ref> ''Star Wars Legends [[Epic Collection]]: The Clone Wars Vol. 1'', which includes "The Artist of Naboo," states that the stories within it take place between the events of ''Attack of the Clones'' and ''Revenge of the Sith'', which took place in [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]] and [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]] respectively.<ref name="Date" />
